prompt: A masterful Dutch Golden Age oil painting from the 1600s, characterized by extreme, dramatic chiaroscuro and intense photorealistic detail. In a pitch-black, atmospheric room, a single, bright, warm candle flame illuminates a highly complex, multi-faceted, ornate silver goblet resting on a rustic oak table. The goblet is intricately detailed with deep three-dimensional engravings, sharp ridges, and protruding jewels. The strong, directional light casts a stark, perfectly flat, two-dimensional black shadow of the goblet against the rough, bare plaster wall behind it. The highly complex 3D information of the ornate silver is entirely lost in the shadow, reduced to a simple, flat silhouette that preserves only the object's essential structural outline. Deep umber and antique gold tones, rich traditional oil paint textures, glowing specular highlights, and subtle craquelure (cracking varnish) on the canvas surface. Absolutely no text, no signatures, no charts, no diagrams, no labels, no numbers, no modern elements.
tradition_approach: Targeted Rembrandt-style dramatic chiaroscuro, photorealistic still-life detail, a single intense directional light source, a deep umber-and-gold palette, and the visual tension between a highly complex 3D silver goblet and its stark, flattened 2D shadow
